subject: Computer Networking
title: From 1G to 4G
description: A quick win: understand From 1G to 4G.

hook.text: In 1983, the first cell phone weighed two pounds and could only make voice calls. That was 1G.

buildup.text: 2G introduced digital voice and text messages in 1991. GSM became the global standard.

discovery.text: 3G arrived in 2001 with data speeds fast enough for basic web browsing and email on a phone.

twist.text: 4G LTE launched around 2010 and delivered broadband-level speeds — streaming, gaming, and video calls went mobile.

climax.text: Each generation took about a decade. Every step brought a 10x speed increase and entirely new use cases.

punchline.text: Each 'G' didn't just add speed. It unlocked new possibilities.

quiz.question: What did 2G introduce that 1G couldn't do?
